description:
A backup-operator wants to prove that the latest “restore” test cycle finished successfully.  
    Your goal is to filter the central log file with a regular expression and write two new
    files that the automated audit system will later pick up.

    Initial state
    1. Log file  
       • Full path: /home/user/logs/backup.log  
       • Already exists and contains many mixed backup / restore events.

    2. Test data  
       • The directory /home/user/sample_backups/ contains a few *.tar.gz files that represent
         the archives which were just restored.

    Desired final state
    1. Directory  
       • /home/user/restore_tests/ must exist (create it if necessary).

    2. Filtered log  
       • File name: /home/user/restore_tests/restore_success.log  
       • Content rule: it must contain **only** the lines from /home/user/logs/backup.log whose
         middle token is the exact string “RESTORE_COMPLETED”.  
       • Keep every such line completely unchanged and in the original order found in
         backup.log.  
       • Do not add header lines, footers, blank lines, or any other text.

       Example of the exact format each kept line already has in backup.log  
       YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=<name> status=OK duration=<Ns>

    3. Numeric summary  
       • File name: /home/user/restore_tests/restore_summary.txt  
       • Content: **one single line** followed by a newline character.  
         Total completed restores: <N>

         where <N> is the number of lines present in restore_success.log after the filter
         operation.

    4. Preservation  
       • Do NOT modify /home/user/logs/backup.log in any way.  
       • Do NOT move or delete the sample *.tar.gz files; copying or listing them is fine.

    What to do (roughly 5–10 commands, order is up to you)
    • Make sure /home/user/restore_tests/ exists.  
    • Use a suitable grep/awk/sed or similar REGEX-based approach to locate the
      “RESTORE_COMPLETED” lines in /home/user/logs/backup.log.  
    • Redirect the matching lines to /home/user/restore_tests/restore_success.log  
    • Count the lines you just wrote and compose /home/user/restore_tests/restore_summary.txt
      exactly as specified.  
    • Optionally inspect /home/user/sample_backups/ to confirm the archives are present (helps
      the operator, but is not graded).

    Verification details the grader will enforce
    • The file /home/user/restore_tests/restore_success.log must exist and contain ONLY the
      correct lines (no extras, no missing lines, no re-ordering, no trailing blank lines).  
    • The file /home/user/restore_tests/restore_summary.txt must exist with the exact single
      line “Total completed restores: <N>” where <N> equals the number of lines in
      restore_success.log.  
    • backup.log must remain unaltered.

    Hint: a simple extended regular expression such as
          “[[:space:]]-[[:space:]]RESTORE_COMPLETED[[:space:]]-”
          will perfectly isolate the desired lines.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing filesystem items
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <directory>/home/user/logs</directory>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/backup.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-10 08:12:30 - START_BACKUP - backup_name=home_full
2023-07-10 09:03:02 - BACKUP_COMPLETED - backup_name=home_full status=OK duration=3032s
2023-07-11 11:00:00 - START_RESTORE - backup_name=home_full
2023-07-11 11:05:22 - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=home_full status=OK duration=322s
2023-07-12 06:45:11 - START_BACKUP - backup_name=db_inc
2023-07-12 06:50:45 - BACKUP_FAILED - backup_name=db_inc status=ERROR code=28
2023-07-12 07:10:00 - START_RESTORE - backup_name=db_inc
2023-07-12 07:15:40 - RESTORE_FAILED - backup_name=db_inc status=ERROR code=28
2023-07-13 14:23:55 - START_RESTORE - backup_name=conf_files
2023-07-13 14:24:05 - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=conf_files status=OK duration=10s
2023-07-14 17:32:01 - START_RESTORE - backup_name=logs_archive
2023-07-14 17:33:59 - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=logs_archive status=OK duration=118s
        ]]></file>

        <directory>/home/user/sample_backups</directory>
        <file path="/home/user/sample_backups/home_full_2023-07-10.tar.gz" />
        <file path="/home/user/sample_backups/conf_files_2023-07-13.tar.gz" />
        <file path="/home/user/sample_backups/logs_archive_2023-07-14.tar.gz" />
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        Files/directories that must exist after the agent finishes
    -->
    <expected>
        <directory>/home/user/restore_tests</directory>

        <file path="/home/user/restore_tests/restore_success.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-11 11:05:22 - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=home_full status=OK duration=322s
2023-07-13 14:24:05 - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=conf_files status=OK duration=10s
2023-07-14 17:33:59 - RESTORE_COMPLETED - backup_name=logs_archive status=OK duration=118s
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/restore_tests/restore_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
Total completed restores: 3
        ]]></file>
    </expected>
